resposta:
solution: 
```
class Solution:
    def canMakePalindromeQueries(self, s: str, queries: List[List[int]]) -> List[bool]:
        n = len(s)
        res = []
        for a, b, c, d in queries:
            left = s[:a][::-1] + s[a:b]
            right = s[c:d][::-1] + s[c:]
            if left == left[::-1] and right == right[::-1]:
                res.append(True)
            else:
                res.append(False)
        return res
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution iterates over each query and rearranges the substrings as specified. It then checks if the rearranged substrings are palindromes. If they are, it adds True to the result list; otherwise, it adds False. This approach has a high efficiency due to its simplicity and the fact that it only needs to check if the substrings are palindromes, which can be done in linear time.
